blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: I've been thinking about the door.
Nobody asked him to continue. He continued anyway.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: The privacy latch. The kind you twist from inside. You can't turn it from outside — there's no mechanism for it. And the window—

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: I need to say this out loud. The window is a twenty-foot drop to the beach. Nobody went out the window. And the door was locked from the inside. So whatever happened to Marcus — nobody did it. Nobody could have gotten in.
The kitchen was quiet. The ocean came through the window screen.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: I think the physical reality of that room makes it extremely difficult to conclude otherwise.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: Then why are we still here? If it was natural why won't they let us leave?

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: Because they have to rule things out formally. That's procedure. It doesn't mean—

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: It means something, Dean. They don't keep five people from leaving a beach house because of procedure.
Dean opened his mouth. Closed it. He didn't have a clean answer for that and everyone in the room knew it.
